summary: Propagated the data-normalize landing (Normalize, Reconstruct, IWriter + json.Writer, Tagged filter, View.Store, [Masked]) into Documentation/Runtime2/data-spec.md §16a + a new good_to_know.md rule. Swept WireJsonConverter → Wire across 6 docs + CLAUDE.md per auditor directive. Fixed stale four-field XML doc on Wire.cs. No missing PLang examples, no unclear-intent code — branch ready to merge.

changes.description: Added §16a 'Wire shape: Normalize → IWriter → bytes' covering Normalize bounds/typed errors, IWriter + json.Writer, Tagged filter, View.Store carve-out, [Masked], Reconstruct<T> dispatch + error keys, and known non-blocking follow-ups. Also swept WireJsonConverter → Wire in §15/§16.
Updated the 'Data is not enveloped' anchor: app.data.WireJsonConverter → app.data.Wire, added the Normalize → IWriter pipeline reference, pointed at the new good_to_know.md rule.
Swept three WireJsonConverter references; added Normalize/Reconstruct file entries to the directory tree.
Renamed Wire converter entry to Wire.cs; added this.Normalize.cs / this.Reconstruct.cs siblings. App-tree checker reports clean.
Swept four WireJsonConverter references; expanded See-also with Normalize/Reconstruct/IWriter/Tagged file pointers; cross-linked §16a in the application/plang section.
Swept WireJsonConverter references in §15 (Transport Pipeline signature round-trip) and §16 (Signature).
Swept WireJsonConverter reference in the Signing — Architecture section.
New rule 'Domain types ride the wire as property bags, not bespoke JSON converters' — captures the new pattern, why, how to apply, and the path inbound carve-out.
Fixed stale class summary: four-field → five-field; documented that the value slot is built via Normalize(View) → IWriter so domain types ride as [Out]-tagged property bags.

findings.issue: Class XML doc summary said canonical four-field shape {name, type, value, signature}; implementation emits five-field shape {name, type, value, properties, signature}.
findings.detail: Aligned the summary with the implementation, also flagged the Normalize+IWriter pipeline so consumers reading the source understand where the value slot comes from.

findings.issue: All references to WireJsonConverter / WireJsonConverter.cs / WireJsonConverter.Write are stale post-data-normalize rename.
findings.detail: Swept all sites to Wire / Wire.cs / Wire.Write with explicit 'renamed from WireJsonConverter on data-normalize' breadcrumbs so a reader landing on old docs still navigates correctly.

findings.issue: No architecture coverage for the structural-normalize pipeline (Normalize, Reconstruct<T>, IWriter, json.Writer, Tagged filter, View.Store, [Masked]).
findings.detail: New §16a added; six new surfaces documented with dispatch tables, error-key matrix, and a 'where to look in code' index.

findings.issue: The 'add [Out], don't add a JsonConverter' rule that flows from the new pipeline is not codified anywhere, so a future contributor adding a wire-bound domain type will repeat the bespoke-converter pattern Normalize is meant to retire.
findings.detail: New rule landed with the smell, the why, how to apply, and the path inbound carve-out as a footnote.

findings.issue: this.Normalize.cs and this.Reconstruct.cs are absent from the tree views; Wire.cs entry shows the old name.
findings.detail: Both files added to both trees; Wire.cs entry renamed; app-tree checker reports clean.

findings.issue: Auditor v1 flagged two minor footguns in Reconstruct (positional-ctor required-param silent default; longest-ctor comment drift).
findings.detail: Documented as 'Known follow-ups' inside the new §16a rather than filed as separate issues — neither has current callers, so a non-blocking note in-place is the right scope.
